segments.text: In the eighth month of the second year of Darius, the word of the Lord came to Zechariah the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo the prophet, saying,
The Lord has been very angry with your fathers.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Therefore say to them,
Thus says the Lord of hosts: Return to Me, and I will return to you.
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: Do not be like your fathers, to whom the former prophets preached: Turn now from your evil ways and your evil deeds. But they did not hear nor heed Me.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Your fathers, where are they? And the prophets, do they live forever?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Yet surely My words and My statutes, Which I commanded My servants the prophets, Did they not overtake your fathers?
Just as the Lord of hosts determined to do to us, According to our ways and according to our deeds, So He has dealt with us.
segments.speaker: The Fathers

segments.text: On the twenty-fourth day of the eleventh month, which is the month Shebat, in the second year of Darius, the word of the Lord came to Zechariah the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo the prophet:
I saw by night, and behold, a man riding on a red horse, and it stood among the myrtle trees in the hollow; and behind him were horses: red, sorrel, and white.
Then I said,
My lord, what are these?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: So the angel who talked with me said to me,
I will show you what they are.
segments.speaker: The Angel

segments.text: And the man who stood among the myrtle trees answered and said,
These are the ones whom the Lord has sent to walk to and fro throughout the earth.
segments.speaker: The Man Among the Myrtle Trees

segments.text: So they answered the Angel of the Lord, who stood among the myrtle trees, and said,
We have walked to and fro throughout the earth, and behold, all the earth is resting quietly.
segments.speaker: The Horsemen

segments.text: Then the Angel of the Lord answered and said,
O Lord of hosts, how long will You not have mercy on Jerusalem and on the cities of Judah, against which You were angry these seventy years?
segments.speaker: The Angel of the Lord

segments.text: And the Lord answered the angel who talked to me, with good and comforting words.
So the angel who spoke with me said to me, 'Proclaim, saying,
I am zealous for Jerusalem And for Zion with great zeal.
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: I am exceedingly angry with the nations at ease; For I was a little angry, And they helped— but with evil intent.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I am returning to Jerusalem with mercy; My house shall be built in it, And a surveyor's line shall be stretched out over Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: My cities shall again spread out through prosperity; The Lord will again comfort Zion, And will again choose Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: Then I raised my eyes and looked, and there were four horns.
And I said to the angel who talked with me,
What are these?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: So he answered me,
These are the horns that have scattered Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: The Angel

segments.text: Then the Lord showed me four craftsmen.
And I said,
What are these coming to do?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: So he said,

These are the horns that scattered Judah, so that no one could lift up his head; but the craftsmen are coming to terrify them, to cast out the horns of the nations that lifted up their horn against the land of Judah to scatter it.
segments.speaker: The Angel
